NVDA.title: Anthropic’s Claude Available in Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) Foundry Powered by Nvidia GPUs
NVDA.summary: Microsoft Corporation has made Anthropic’s Claude models generally available in its Foundry, allowing organizations to integrate Claude into their Azure environments. These deployments leverage Azure infrastructure powered by Nvidia’s GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs, stemming from a partnership between Microsoft, Nvidia, and Anthropic. This development enables enterprises to build agentic applications within their familiar Microsoft Azure ecosystem, marking a significant step from AI experimentation to production.

NVDA.title: AMD Stock and Intel Crushed Nvidia in the First Half. Here's My Prediction for the Second Half.
NVDA.summary: Nvidia (NVDA) significantly outperformed other AI stocks for years but saw a shift in the first half of this year, with investors rotating to Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC), causing them to soar while Nvidia had a modest gain. The author predicts Nvidia will outpace AMD and Intel in the second half due to its plans to dominate the CPU market with new releases and its attractive valuation. Nvidia is launching its first stand-alone CPU and a superchip this fall, and its stock is currently valued at about 22x forward earnings, appearing "dirt cheap" compared to its peers.

NVDA.title: Western Asset Diversified Income Fund Q1 2026 Commentary
NVDA.summary: The Western Asset Diversified Income Fund's Q1 2026 performance was significantly boosted by high-yield corporates and bank loans but detracted by its duration and yield-curve positioning as US Treasury yields increased. Credit markets remain strong, supported by demand and elevated issuance driven by AI-related capital expenditures, mergers, and refinancing needs. The outlook is shaped by geopolitical risks and accelerating AI disruption.

NVDA.title: Micron (MU) Stock Faces Michael Burry Short As Legal Risks Linger
NVDA.summary: Michael Burry has opened a short position against Micron Technology (MU) despite the company reporting record earnings driven by AI memory demand. This move adds to the stock's volatility, which is already influenced by an ongoing class-action lawsuit alleging price fixing and recent insider share sales. Micron's stock performance has been exceptional, but Burry's short, coupled with existing legal and valuation concerns, suggests potential future challenges for the company.

NVDA.title: Nvidia supplier Hon Hai’s sales beat on continued AI demand
NVDA.summary: Nvidia supplier Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. (Foxconn) reported a stronger-than-expected 40% increase in quarterly sales, driven by sustained demand for AI-related products. The company anticipates continued momentum in AI server shipments and overall growth quarter-on-quarter and year-on-year, despite facing a shortage of memory chips. This performance comes as major tech companies like Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft are significantly increasing their AI investments.

NVDA.title: Enphase Energy signs $52M safe harbor deal with solar financier
NVDA.summary: Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) has secured a $52 million safe harbor agreement with a U.S. solar and battery financing company to supply IQ9 Microinverters, contributing to a total backlog of $873.7 million. This deal helps future projects retain eligibility for investment tax credits. Despite near-term sales concerns from analysts, InvestingPro data suggests the stock is undervalued, while recent analyst adjustments reflect mixed perspectives on the company's outlook.

NVDA.title: American Express (AXP) Stock Gains Momentum as Analysts Raise Price Targets
NVDA.summary: American Express (AXP) stock is gaining momentum, trading near $351.96 after exceeding first-quarter 2026 earnings expectations and receiving multiple analyst price target increases. The company's growth is significantly driven by its success in attracting younger demographics like Millennials and Gen Z, who are engaging with premium card offerings. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, expecting continued earnings expansion and reasonable valuation metrics.

NVDA.title: Opendoor stock turnover signals Russell index demand test after 12% gain during holiday week
NVDA.summary: Opendoor Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:OPEN) saw a 12.1% stock gain in the four sessions following its inclusion in the Russell 3000 Index, accompanied by high trading volume representing 43% of its public float. The article discusses whether this initial index-driven demand will sustain after the holiday-shortened week, emphasizing the importance of Monday's trading to determine a new support level for the stock and its ability to break past the $5.17 resistance. Despite the stock boost, Opendoor is in a turnaround phase, reporting a Q1 net loss of $173 million but expecting 25% revenue growth in Q2.

MSFT.title: Anthropic’s Claude Available in Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) Foundry Powered by Nvidia GPUs
MSFT.summary: Microsoft Corporation has made Anthropic’s Claude models generally available in its Foundry, allowing organizations to integrate Claude into their Azure environments. These deployments leverage Azure infrastructure powered by Nvidia’s GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs, stemming from a partnership between Microsoft, Nvidia, and Anthropic. This development enables enterprises to build agentic applications within their familiar Microsoft Azure ecosystem, marking a significant step from AI experimentation to production.

MSFT.title: Constellation Energy Stock analysis: Is CEG a strong AI data centre power demand trade idea?
MSFT.summary: This article analyzes Constellation Energy (CEG) as a potential trade idea driven by AI data center power demand. It highlights Constellation's strategic position as the largest nuclear operator in the US, crucial for supplying reliable, clean electricity to power-intensive AI data centers. While the long-term fundamentals and existing contracts with tech giants like Microsoft and Meta support the thesis, the article notes that sentiment has cooled, the stock's valuation isn't cheap, and technicals currently show weakness, suggesting it's a strong thematic play but not a short-term momentum trade.

MSFT.title: Wisconsin energy demand to rise 40% by 2032, driven by data centers
MSFT.summary: Wisconsin's energy demand is projected to increase by 40% by 2032, primarily due to the development of new hyperscale artificial intelligence data centers in the southern part of the state, including projects by Microsoft, Meta, Oracle, and OpenAI. This surge in demand necessitates significant investments in new energy supply and infrastructure, with utility companies planning billions in solar, wind, battery storage, and natural gas projects. The report also highlights a continued shift away from coal power towards natural gas and renewables, despite some delays in coal plant closures to meet immediate energy needs.

MSFT.title: Western Asset Diversified Income Fund Q1 2026 Commentary
MSFT.summary: The Western Asset Diversified Income Fund's Q1 2026 performance was significantly boosted by high-yield corporates and bank loans but detracted by its duration and yield-curve positioning as US Treasury yields increased. Credit markets remain strong, supported by demand and elevated issuance driven by AI-related capital expenditures, mergers, and refinancing needs. The outlook is shaped by geopolitical risks and accelerating AI disruption.

MSFT.summary: Nvidia supplier Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. (Foxconn) reported a stronger-than-expected 40% increase in quarterly sales, driven by sustained demand for AI-related products. The company anticipates continued momentum in AI server shipments and overall growth quarter-on-quarter and year-on-year, despite facing a shortage of memory chips. This performance comes as major tech companies like Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft are significantly increasing their AI investments.

MSFT.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Microsoft Corporation Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
MSFT.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT) and its officers, alleging federal securities law violations. The lawsuit claims Microsoft made misleading statements regarding its Copilot products, AI model performance, and increased capital expenditures required to address competitive shortcomings. Investors who purchased Microsoft securities between May 1, 2025, and January 28, 2026, are encouraged to join the case, with an August 11, 2026 deadline to request lead plaintiff status.

MSFT.title: AECOM (ACM) On Russell Index Removal And Whether Its Value Case Still Holds
MSFT.summary: AECOM (ACM) has been removed from the Russell 1000 Defensive and Value Defensive Indexes, prompting increased investor scrutiny. Despite a recent share price decline and weakened momentum, the company is considered 36.6% undervalued at $67.74 with a fair value estimate of $106.88, driven by expectations of government-backed infrastructure spending and a strong backlog. Investors are advised to weigh potential upsides against risks such as dependence on government budgets and complex projects.

MSFT.title: Walter Public Investments Inc. Acquires 84,763 Shares of Toronto Dominion Bank (The) $TD
MSFT.summary: Walter Public Investments Inc. significantly increased its stake in Toronto Dominion Bank (NYSE:TD) by acquiring an additional 84,763 shares, bringing its total holdings to 93,763 shares valued at approximately $8.76 million. The bank also reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings of $1.74 per share, raised its quarterly dividend to $1.12, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $156. Institutional investors collectively hold 52.37% of Toronto Dominion Bank's stock.

GOOGL.title: Nvidia supplier Hon Hai’s sales beat on continued AI demand
GOOGL.summary: Nvidia supplier Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. (Foxconn) reported a stronger-than-expected 40% increase in quarterly sales, driven by sustained demand for AI-related products. The company anticipates continued momentum in AI server shipments and overall growth quarter-on-quarter and year-on-year, despite facing a shortage of memory chips. This performance comes as major tech companies like Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft are significantly increasing their AI investments.

GOOGL.title: KKR & Co. Inc. $KKR Shares Bought by Fjarde AP Fonden Fourth Swedish National Pension Fund
GOOGL.summary: Fjarde AP Fonden Fourth Swedish National Pension Fund increased its stake in KKR & Co. Inc. by 54.8% in the first quarter, acquiring 53,700 additional shares to own a total of 151,675 shares valued at $14.03 million. KKR & Co. Inc. recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.195 per share and approved a $500 million share buyback program. Despite analyst price target reductions, the stock maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $134.53.

GOOGL.title: Louisiana State Employees Retirement System Purchases New Shares in VeriSign, Inc. $VRSN
GOOGL.summary: The Louisiana State Employees Retirement System has acquired 4,200 shares of VeriSign, Inc. (NASDAQ:VRSN) for approximately $1.043 million during the first quarter. This purchase contributes to the already high institutional ownership of 92.9% for VeriSign shares. The company recently reported strong quarterly results, surpassing EPS expectations with $2.34 per share and revenue of $428.9 million, leading analysts to rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a target price of $327.67.

GOOGL.title: Is Arista Networks (ANET) The Best AI Stock to Buy?
GOOGL.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) is presented as a strong contender in the AI market, acting as the connectivity backbone for AI infrastructure in data centers. The company shows robust financial performance with high revenue and EPS growth, despite a premium valuation. Its future prospects are tied to the increasing demand for networking solutions as AI workloads shift towards more inference and require more interconnected hardware.

GOOGL.title: Intuit Inc. $INTU Shares Sold by FUKOKU MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE Co
GOOGL.summary: FUKOKU MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE Co significantly reduced its stake in Intuit Inc. (INTU) by 87% in the first quarter, selling 6,000 shares and retaining 896 shares valued at approximately $387,000. Despite this, analyst sentiment remains largely positive with a "Moderate Buy" consensus and an average target price of $498.40. Intuit recently reported strong quarterly results, beating EPS and revenue estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $1.20 per share.

GOOGL.title: Border to Coast Pensions Partnership Ltd Raises Stock Position in Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN
GOOGL.summary: Border to Coast Pensions Partnership Ltd increased its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. by 2.4% in the first quarter, bringing its total holdings to 989,296 shares valued at approximately $206.7 million. Amazon is now the fund's 7th largest position, comprising 3.5% of its portfolio. The article also highlights Amazon's strong first-quarter earnings, new growth drivers including its Leo satellite network and expanded AI efforts, and a positive outlook from Wall Street analysts with a consensus "Buy" rating and a target price of $312.79.

GOOGL.title: Baer Investment Advisory LLC Reduces Stock Holdings in Intuit Inc. $INTU
GOOGL.summary: Baer Investment Advisory LLC decreased its stake in Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ:INTU) by 72.2% in the first quarter, now holding 2,029 shares valued at $877,000. This reduction comes despite Intuit reporting strong quarterly results with EPS of $12.80 and revenue of $8.56 billion, exceeding analyst expectations. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.20 per share and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, although several firms have recently lowered their price targets.

GOOGL.title: Data Center Leases Top $850 Billion as Meta, Microsoft, Oracle Lead Record AI Infrastructure Spending
GOOGL.summary: U.S. tech companies, led by Meta, Microsoft, and Oracle, committed over $850 billion to data center leases in Q1 2026, marking a record high for AI infrastructure spending. This represents a 204% increase year-over-year and a 31% increase from the previous quarter. The surge in spending occurs despite growing community opposition related to water use, power costs, and pollution, with 71% of Americans opposing data centers near their homes and an estimated $130 billion in projects being delayed or blocked in Q1 2026.
